Defendant American Guarantee And Liability Insurance Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Of Defendant American Guarantee And Liabiltiy Insurance Company
Off Calendar Mar-18-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOEL P. GUMBINER & ATTY. GORDON D. MCAUlEY OF WILLIAMS & GUMBINER LLP, TEL. NO. 755-1880 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JONATHAN GROSS OF THE MOUND COTTON WOLLAN & GREEBGRASS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (510)900-9371 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant American Guarantee And Liability Insurance Company's Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication - The court grants defendant's motion for summary judgment. The court grants as to issue 1 [building damage]. Defendant's motion shows that Columbus Foods, LLC ("LLC") purchased the insurance and it was listed as the first named insured. LLC thus facially appears to be entitled to coverage and defendant properly paid sums to it. In opposition, plaintiff argues that LLC had no insurable interest, but fails to present evidence demonstrating that fact. As an aside, defendant's opposition to plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication shows that LLC did in fact have an insurable interest within the meaning of Cal. Food Service Corp. v. Great American Ins. Co. (1982) 130 Cal.App.3d 892, 897. (See Ferrif Dec.). The court grants as to issue 2 [demolition]. Mr. Boll worked for Stellar and estimated the replacement cost of the building. At 90:12-16 of the Boll Depo, he indicates that the demolition costs were included in the replacement estimate. The court grants as to issue 3 [professional fees]. Plaintiff admits that plaintiff and LLC jointly retained Dempsey Partners to help present their claims. (Defendant's UMF 15). The undisputed evidence shows that there is a $100,000 limit for such professional fees and defendant paid that sum to LLC. The court grants as to issues 4 and 5 [causes of action] based on the above. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF DGP ASSOCIATES, L.P.'s Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff is granted leave to file its Proposed First Amended Complaint (FAC). (See CCP 473(a)(1); see also Guidery v. Green, 95 Cal. 630, 633 [rarely is a court "justified in refusing a party LTA his pleadings so that he may properly present his case"]. Defendant has not argued that Plaintiff's Motion is untimely or that it will suffer any prejudice if the Motion is granted. (Morgan v. Sup. Ct. of Calif. (1959) 172 Cal.App.2d 527, 530 ["If the motion to amend is timely made and the granting of the motion will not prejudice the opposing party, it is error to refuse permission to amend . . . ."].) Plaintiff's requests to have the Proposed FAC deemed filed and served and the original Answers deemed the Answers to the FAC are denied. Plaintiff must file and serve the FAC pursuant to statute and local rules, and Defendants are entitled to file any responsive pleadings permitted by statute. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
